qa_pairs.question: When is the deadline for submitting proposals for the Resource Allocation Competition, and how are they submitted?
qa_pairs.answer: Proposals for the Resource Allocation Competition must be submitted electronically through the CCDB portal no later than November 4, 2025, at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Standard Time. Extensions to this deadline are not possible.

qa_pairs.question: What are the requirements for the Canadian Common CV (CCV) in a Resource Allocation Competition application, and what are the consequences of not meeting them?
qa_pairs.answer: An up-to-date Canadian Common CV (CCV) is required for both the Principal Investigator and all Co-Principal Investigators for the peer review process. Failure to provide an updated CCV will negatively impact the overall score of the submitted application, and deadline extensions due to missing CCVs are not possible.

qa_pairs.question: What is the role of the technical review in the Resource Allocation Competition assessment process?
qa_pairs.answer: The technical review, conducted by Federation technical experts, ensures the appropriate system and software are requested, evaluates application efficiency and scalability, identifies groups needing workflow optimization help, checks for discrepancies between the online form and the application document, identifies special software requirements, and provides a technical opinion on the reasonability of the resource request. Applicants are expected to respond to staff requests for additional information within 48 hours during this process.
